description_long: This object is a sleek, unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) with a long, slender fuselage and straight, narrow wings extending symmetrically from the midsection. The wings are mounted in a mid-wing configuration and feature no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral angles. The nose is rounded and tapers smoothly, while the tail section is equipped with a distinctive inverted V-tail (also known as a butterfly tail) and twin vertical stabilizers. The undercarriage consists of a tricycle landing gear arrangement, with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, all of which appear to be fixed. The surface is uniformly dark, likely matte or semi-matte, and the overall design is optimized for aerodynamic efficiency and minimal radar signature.
description: A dark, streamlined unmanned aerial vehicle with straight mid-mounted wings, an inverted V-tail, and fixed tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow fuselage, straight wings, and the inverted V-tail configuration clearly visible behind the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated nose, mid-mounted wings, and the fixed tricycle landing gear beneath the fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full span of the wings and the distinctive inverted V-tail with twin vertical stabilizers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with the landing gear and tail configuration visible from the other side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings, no visible winglets, and no pronounced dihedral or anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single pusher propeller engine mounted at the rear of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Inverted V-tail (butterfly tail) with twin vertical stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and one nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single-seat configuration and a streamlined canopy.
